summary: Germany pledged €300 million for 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine. Ukrainian forces suffered civilian casualties in Kharkiv, including three children, while Russian air defenses reported downing 292 Ukrainian UAVs in one day. Drone attacks intensified across the front, with 140 UAVs destroyed over Russian regions and strikes on Sevastopol and Crimea.
key_points: Germany will allocate an additional €300 million to deliver 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30794).
In Kharkiv, 15 civilians were killed, including three children, after Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
Russian air defence claimed to have shot down 292 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Ukrainian drones attacked Sevastopol and Crimea, with 140 UAVs destroyed over multiple Russian regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian forces struck a gas station in Kherson region, killing one man (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
A U.S. Apache combat helicopter crashed near the Ormuz Strait (Source: @tass_agency/379936).
Russian UAV groups from Belgorod and other oblasts conducted strikes on villages in Belgorod region, causing civilian injuries (Source: @kpszsu/64622).

sections.executive_summary: Germany pledged €300 million for 50,000 long‑range munitions to Ukraine. Ukrainian civilian casualties rose in Kharkiv, with 15 deaths including three children. Drone warfare escalated, with 140 Ukrainian UAVs destroyed over Russian territory and Russian air defences downing 292 UAVs in one day.

points: Kharkiv suffered 15 civilian deaths, among them three children aged 11, 16 and one infant, after Russian attacks on the city (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
Ukrainian forces attacked a gas station in the Kherson region, killing a man while the station was serving a queue of vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
In Kupyansk, small Russian groups infiltrated the southeast of the city, prompting Ukrainian troops to conduct active operations with tactical UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian troops fought in the center of Krasnyi Liman and around the Liman railway station, facing infantry infiltrations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Power outages persisted across many settlements in the Zaporozhye region due to continuous Ukrainian attacks on infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, Nova Sich and surrounding villages on the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 292 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day, according to the Russian Ministry of Defence (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
A U.S. Apache combat helicopter crashed near the Ormuz Strait, as reported by the New York Times (Source: @tass_agency/379936).
Russian forces used FPV drones to strike civilian targets in Belgorod region, injuring two people in Borisovka and a 14‑year‑old boy in Dunayka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian authorities announced fines up to 700 000 rubles for using foreign online services, reflecting tightening internal security measures (Source: @tass_agency/379944).
Russian officials reported that nine trains to Crimea were delayed after nighttime UAV attacks on the peninsula (Source: @tass_agency/379934).
Russian forces claimed liberation of the Khimik (Molocharka) settlement northeast of Konstantinovka, with intense urban combat ongoing (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
In the Shostka district of Sumy, Ukrainian forces engaged Russian assault aircraft in firefights around Bachevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian troops advanced in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka and Nova Sich, pushing Russian lines on the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian attacks hit Kharkiv, damaging a dozen administrative and production premises, according to the city administration (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian forces reported penetrating Russian defenses northwest of Alexandrograd in the Dnipropetrovsk region, operating beyond the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Small Russian groups infiltrated the southeast of Kupyansk, prompting Ukrainian counter‑operations with tactical UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian UAVs launched from Belgorod oblast headed north toward Kharkiv region, as indicated by multiple intelligence posts (Source: @kpszsu/64602).
UAV groups from northern Kharkiv moved toward Slatyne, Vylshany and the settlement Vysokyi (Source: @kpszsu/64616).
UAVs from northern Chernihiv flew along the Belarus border toward northern Kyiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64627).
UAV activity reported along the Chernihiv‑Sumy border moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/64629).
Ukrainian reconnaissance reported a UAV near Kam'yanka north of Romny in Sumy oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/96025).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Ulyankivka in Sumy and Shevchenko in Kharkiv (Source: @eRadarrua/96003).
Ukrainian forces attacked a gas station in Kherson region, killing one civilian during a queue for fuel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
The Chongar Bridge to Crimea was damaged again after Ukrainian forces struck it following the resumption of reverse traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian UAVs struck villages in the Zaporozhye region, causing prolonged power outages and civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian UAV attacks in the Grayvoron district of Belgorod injured civilians in Glotovo, Belovskoe and Dunayka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian UAV groups from southern Zaporizhzhia oblast moved toward the city of Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/64625).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near Pavlograd heading toward Ternivka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/64599).

Ukrainian drones attacked Sevastopol and Crimea, targeting energy and logistics infrastructure; footage of a new strike UAV "Behemoth" (range up to 300 km, speed 200 km/h, payload 75 kg) was released (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Over the night, 140 UAVs were destroyed over Belgorod, Bryansk, Kaluga, Kursk, Orel, Tula, Moscow region, Crimea and the Azov and Black Seas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian air defence claimed to have shot down 292 Ukrainian UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
FPV drones were used by Russian forces to strike civilian structures in Belgorod villages, causing injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Ukrainian UAV groups operated from multiple Russian oblasts, including Belgorod, Sumy and Kharkiv, targeting forward positions (Source: @kpszsu/64622).
A U.S. Apache combat helicopter crashed near the Ormuz Strait, highlighting risks to rotary‑wing assets (Source: @tass_agency/379936).

key_developments: Germany pledged €300 million to supply Ukraine with 50,000 long‑range munitions, expanding Kyiv's strike capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30794).
Russian air defence reported downing 292 Ukrainian UAVs in one day, indicating intensified aerial contest (Source: @tass_agency/379946).
Ukrainian drone attacks on Sevastopol and Crimea employed the new "Behemoth" UAV, with 140 UAVs destroyed across Russian regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Civilian casualties in Kharkiv rose to 15, including three children, after Russian shelling (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
Ukrainian forces struck a Kherson gas station, killing a civilian during a fuel queue (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
Russian FPV drones hit civilian targets in Belgorod region, injuring multiple residents (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780).
A U.S. Apache helicopter crashed near the Ormuz Strait, underscoring operational hazards for allied air assets (Source: @tass_agency/379936).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian offensive capabilities are being bolstered by German long‑range munitions, while Russian air defences claim high UAV attrition rates, suggesting a contested airspace. Drone warfare remains a central element, with both sides employing UAVs for strike and reconnaissance, leading to civilian infrastructure damage in contested regions. Russian ground infiltrations in Kupyansk and Krasnyi Liman indicate continued attempts to exploit gaps in Ukrainian lines, while Ukrainian advances in Sumy and Kharkiv pressure Russian defensive depth.
sections.regional_response: Western allies, exemplified by Germany's financial commitment, are increasing long‑range strike support, whereas Russia is tightening internal security and imposing fines for foreign service use. Neighboring states monitor the escalation, with reports of train disruptions to Crimea following UAV attacks, reflecting broader regional transport impacts. The intensity of civilian casualties and infrastructure attacks may prompt heightened humanitarian assistance and evacuation measures in affected oblasts.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV activity patterns show a north‑south corridor from Belgorod into Kharkiv and a south‑west flow from Zaporizhzhia toward the Crimean bridge, suggesting coordinated strike corridors. The reported loss of an Apache helicopter near Ormuz highlights the vulnerability of allied rotary assets in contested maritime zones. Continued Russian claims of high UAV shoot‑down numbers may be inflated; independent verification is needed to assess actual air superiority dynamics.
